headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Morgan Stanley (MS) After Its Strong One Year Rally?
summary: Investors may be wondering if Morgan Stanley at around US$197.77 per share still offers value, or if most of the upside is already reflected in the stock. The stock shows returns of 2.0% over 7 days, 3.7% over 30 days, 8.7% year to date, 61.6% over 1 year and 160.0% over 5 years, which has put valuation firmly on many investors' radar. Recent coverage has focused on Morgan Stanley's position as a global financial institution and how investor sentiment around large US financial stocks has...

headline: Peak valuation leaves IT hardware vulnerable to downgrades, Morgan Stanley warns

headline: Tesla Rival Li Auto's Stock Remains Underpriced As Value Score Rises— Morgan Stanley Sees 61% Upside Potential
summary: Shares of Tesla Inc.‘s (NASDAQ:TSLA) Chinese rival Li Auto Inc. (NASDAQ:LI) have seen its Benzinga Edge value score surge to 93.52 percentile, revealing a glaring disconnect between its battered equity price and fundamental worth. Deep Fundamental Value Amid Market Selloff While short, medium, and long-term price trends remain heavily pressured, value score expansion comes after a punishing year for the EV manufacturer, with shares tumbling 42.73% over the past 12 months. Benzinga Edge Stock Ran

headline: SpaceX Plans Its ‘Money Product’ and Other Banking Takeaways From IPO Filing
summary: The details that Elon Musk’s SpaceX outlined about its initial public offering this week showcase the rocket company’s ties across Wall Street, and its own ambitions in the financial sector.  SpaceX owns xAI, Musk’s artificial-intelligence start-up, which in turn owns X, formerly known as Twitter.  It may expand into other financial offerings, SpaceX wrote in the filing.

SpaceX owns xAI, Musk’s artificial-intelligence start-up, which in turn owns X, formerly known as Twitter.  It may expand into other financial offerings, SpaceX wrote in the filing.

headline: Hour Children and Mental Health Innovations Teams Win Morgan Stanley’s 2026 Strategy Challenge Supporting Nonprofits in the U.S. and the UK
summary: NEW YORK, May 21, 2026--Morgan Stanley (NYSE: MS) today announced the winners of its 17th annual Strategy Challenge, the Firm’s flagship pro bono program which brings together rising talent to help nonprofits solve strategic, mission-critical challenges. The winning teams supported Hour Children in the U.S. and Mental Health Innovations in the UK.
